< back

getpost

End-to-end encrypted file sharing on Cloudflare Workers. No accounts, no tracking, globally distributed.

All uploads are encrypted in your browser before leaving your device. The server never sees your plaintext or your passphrase.

web upload

Drop a file on the main page. A passphrase is generated automatically. Share the resulting link — the passphrase is embedded in the URL fragment (never sent to the server).

command line

# basic upload
curl --data-binary @myfile.txt ${url.toString()}

# upload from clipboard (macOS)
pbpaste | curl --data-binary @- ${url.toString()}

# custom expiration (1 hour)
curl -H "X-TTL: 3600" --data-binary @file.txt ${url.toString()}

CLI uploads are stored unencrypted unless you encrypt the file yourself before uploading.

one-liner script

Save as /usr/local/bin/pastebin and make executable:

#!/bin/bash
curl --data-binary @$\{1:--\} ${url.toString()}

Usage: pastebin myfile.txt or echo "hello" | pastebin

features

  • E2E encryption — NaCl SecretBox (XSalsa20-Poly1305) + Argon2id key derivation
  • Text & markdown rendered automatically
  • Images, PDFs, videos, any file up to 10 MB
  • Append &raw to any link for the original file
  • Default expiry: 1 year, configurable via X-TTL header (seconds)
  • Every upload gets a unique delete key

headers & parameters

# custom expiration
X-TTL: 3600          # seconds until expiry

# URL parameters
?raw                 # return original file
?cors=1              # enable CORS headers

# delete a post (deletion requires POST; GET shows a confirmation page)
curl -X POST "https://your.domain/post?key=KEY&del=DELETE_KEY"

deploy your own

GetPost runs on Cloudflare Workers — zero servers, global distribution, generous free tier (100k reads, 1k uploads daily).

  • Clone: git clone https://github.com/getpost-loves-you/getpost
  • Setup: follow SETUP.md
  • Deploy: ./deploy.sh mydomain

technical details

  • Architecture: Cloudflare Workers + KV storage, edge computing
  • Encryption: NaCl SecretBox + Argon2id (4 iterations, 64 MB memory)
  • IDs: ULID-based, separate delete tokens
  • Performance: sub-100ms response times worldwide

CC0 — No Rights Reserved. source
